question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the importer to emit a very large number of Prometheus series (one per transaction), causing `parseLagByCluster()` to process an unbounded list and consume excessive memory or CPU in the monitor process, leading to a denial-of-service condition?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the importer to emit a Prometheus series with a `cluster` label value that is an empty string or whitespace, which `StringUtils.trimToNull()` in `toClusterLagEntry()` converts to null, causing that series to be silently dropped and potentially hiding lag data for a cluster?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the importer to emit a peer cluster lag value of exactly `localLag - thresholdSeconds`, exploiting the strict `<` comparison in `isOtherClearlyBetter()` to keep the health indicator returning `up()` even when the peer cluster is marginally better?
